definition: The dollar's position on the 'dollar smile' maps to correlation regime states: left side (risk-off) corresponds to negative correlation, middle (muddling) to oscillating, right side (US exceptionalism) to positive correlation. DXY at 100-107 maps to middle-to-right, consistent with the transitional regime.
evidence: DXY at 100-107 consistent with transitional regime diagnosis
Recession-driven vs. twin-deficit-driven dollar weakness provides real-time discriminator
Dollar dynamics identified as material analytical gap in equity_analyst_01's framework
open_questions: The specific correlation mapping adds judgment beyond the established framework
